hypotheses.text: Auth slowness caused by N+1 DB queries in tax calculation
hypotheses.tested: Ran query profiler across 1000 checkout requests
hypotheses.outcome: Confirmed — 47% of all queries were redundant FK lookups

hypotheses.text: Connection pool exhaustion is a secondary factor
hypotheses.tested: Monitored pool wait time under load
hypotheses.outcome: Refuted — pool wait time averaged 2ms, negligible

findings.heading: N+1 pattern confirmed in ORM layer
findings.detail: Each checkout line item triggers a separate SELECT for its tax_rate relationship.
findings.evidence: profiler: avg 847ms DB calls, 803ms attributable to tax_rate lookups

critical.issue: Profiler not accounting for pool overhead

methodological.issue: Sample size of 100 may not be representative

enhancement.issue: Could benchmark Redis cache as alternative

domainInterpretation: The N+1 pattern is a classic Django ORM pitfall when accessing related models in a loop.
futureDirections: Add query count assertion to tests
